title: Error: failed to open 16384 locks in /libpod_rootless_lock_1000: numerical result out of range
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have been doing some performance testing where I needed _many_ containers, and I think this is a bug and/or misleading error message in Podman. Default limit in `engine.num_locks` is `2048` and each container use a lock.
When starting container 2049 with default configuration the following error is displayed:
```
Error: error allocating lock for new container: allocation failed; exceeded num_locks (2048)
```
The issue happens when trying to increase the lock limit.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start one or more container(s)
```
#!/bin/bash

COUNT=2500

for i in $(seq "$COUNT"); do
    podman run --name "client-${i}" -d ubi9/ubi-init
    sleep 2
done
```
3. Stop all container(s)
4. Increase `engine.num_locks` to `16384` in `~/.config/containers/containers.conf`
```
[engine]
num_locks = 16384
```
6. Run `podman system renumber` or `podman system df`

**Describe the results you received:**

Received the following error:

```
Error: failed to open 16384 locks in /libpod_rootless_lock_1000: numerical result out of range
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect `podman system renumber` to remove the `libpod_rootless_lock_1000` file which is too small and create a new one with the correct size.

It took me a little time to figure out that Podman was referring to a file in `/dev/shm`, so maybe add full path to the rootless lock file in error message.

Manually workaround:
```
rm /dev/shm/libpod_rootless_lock_1000
```

A more generic workaround:
```
rm "/dev/shm/libpod_rootless_lock_$(id -u)"
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.18.5
Built:        Wed Sep  7 21:58:19 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes, `v4.2.1` is the latest release at the moment and there is no information in the troubleshooting guide.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 36 on physical Intel.

comments.body:
`system renumber` should be removing the lock manager on ERANGE: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/runtime.go#L276-L284

I know this code works, otherwise `system renumber` would be completely broken...

Can you attach the output of `podman system renumber --log-level=debug` after changing num_locks (when it errors)?
Also - does it work if you use a lower number than 16k? Does 8192 cause similar issues?

Finally: I see you mentioning `system df`. To be clear - `podman system renumber` is absolutely required after changes to the number of locks, so if this error occurs on a `system df` after you change the number of locks that is 100% expected.

Yes, I hit the issue with multiple values like `8192` and `2049`.

Just tried to recreate it on another F36 machine with `podman system renumber --log-level=debug`.
```
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called renumber.PersistentPreRunE(podman system renumber --log-level=debug) 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/home/bruno/.config/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"              
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/bruno/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/bruno/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000                
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/bruno/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/bruno/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] Not configuring container store              
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runj initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runj: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/crun"            
Error: failed to open 2049 locks in /libpod_rootless_lock_1000: numerical result out of range
```
`stat /dev/shm/libpod_rootless_lock_1000`
```
  File: /dev/shm/libpod_rootless_lock_1000
  Size: 82488     	Blocks: 168        IO Block: 4096   regular file
Device: 0,25	Inode: 346         Links: 1
Access: (0600/-rw-------)  Uid: ( 1000/   bruno)   Gid: ( 1000/   bruno)
Context: unconfined_u:object_r:container_runtime_tmpfs_t:s0
Access: 2022-10-11 20:40:52.074824193 +0200
Modify: 2022-10-11 20:27:07.181888325 +0200
Change: 2022-10-11 20:27:07.181888325 +0200
 Birth: 2022-10-11 20:27:07.181888325 +0200
```

The extra `system df` was called after `system renumber` to check if podman commands was working again, but all commands fails until `engine.num_locks` is removed from `~/.config/containers/containers.conf`.
@mheon The problem is in the setup code, before we create the renumber runtime it will first try to create a "regular" runtime and this fails.
Well that's not good... If renumber is requested we need to not do that. Have a link to this, before I go searching in the init code?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a344928c016f301eb6fa99d88009649ed9910cb2/cmd/podman/root.go#L237-L243

This will always run before the main logic in the commands. 
You can see that the first step here is to shut down the old runtime/engine:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a344928c016f301eb6fa99d88009649ed9910cb2/cmd/podman/system/renumber.go#L45-L54
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon Did you ever get a chance to look into this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
